about.title: About Us
about.subtitle: The Department of Library Science at Chulalongkorn University has been a leading center for information studies in Thailand.
about.missionText: To advance the study and practice of information science, library science, and data curation through innovative research, quality education, and community engagement.
about.visionText: To be a leading academic institution in Southeast Asia for information studies, shaping the future of knowledge management and digital scholarship.

bachelor.title: Bachelor of Arts in Information Studies
bachelor.subtitle: A comprehensive undergraduate program preparing students for careers in information management, digital curation, and knowledge services.
bachelor.overviewText: The B.A. in Information Studies is a 4-year program designed to equip students with the knowledge and skills necessary for managing information resources in various organizational settings. The curriculum covers information organization, technology, research methods, and professional practice.
bachelor.admissionReq2: CU-TEP or equivalent English proficiency score

graduateProg.title: Graduate Programs (MA/PhD)
graduateProg.subtitle: Advanced study and research opportunities in information studies, data curation, and related fields.
graduateProg.overviewText: Our graduate programs offer in-depth study of information science, preparing students for leadership roles in academic, government, and private sector institutions. The MA program focuses on professional practice and applied research, while the PhD program emphasizes original scholarly contributions.
graduateProg.research0Desc: Long-term management, preservation, and accessibility of digital and physical data collections.
graduateProg.research1Desc: Applying computational methods to humanities research, including text mining and digital archives.
graduateProg.research2Desc: Understanding how people seek, use, and share information in various contexts.
graduateProg.research3Desc: Classification systems, metadata standards, and ontologies for organizing information.
graduateProg.admissionReq0: Bachelor's degree (MA) or Master's degree (PhD) in a relevant field

blog.title: News & Announcements
blog.subtitle: Stay updated with the latest news, research updates, and announcements from the Department of Library Science.
blog.post0Desc: The department launches a new research unit focusing on digital information landscapes and data curation.
blog.post1Desc: Join us for the annual conference featuring keynote speakers from leading universities worldwide.
blog.post2Desc: Three graduate students recognized for outstanding research in digital preservation and knowledge organization.
blog.post3Desc: The department introduces new courses in AI-powered information retrieval and data analytics.
blog.post4Title: Partnership with National Library of Thailand
blog.post4Desc: A new collaboration to digitize and preserve rare manuscripts and historical documents.
blog.post5Desc: Department alumni share how their education prepared them for careers in digital transformation.

blogPost.paragraph0: Information studies is a field that focuses on the processes of managing, storing, retrieving, and disseminating information in various forms, both digital and physical, with the goal of developing the knowledge and skills necessary for working in the digital age.
blogPost.paragraph1: The Department of Library Science, Faculty of Arts, Chulalongkorn University, was established to produce graduates with knowledge and ability in information management, digital libraries, and related research.
blogPost.paragraph2: With advances in technology and changes in the information landscape, the department is committed to continually developing its curriculum and research to meet the needs of society and industry.

classReview.title: Class Reviews
classReview.subtitle: Read honest reviews from fellow students to help you choose the right courses.
classReview.review0Desc: Great introductory course! The professor explains complex concepts clearly and the assignments are practical and engaging.
classReview.review1Desc: Challenging but rewarding. Learned a lot about metadata standards and classification systems. Highly recommended.
classReview.review2Desc: Hands-on experience with digital library systems. The group project was especially valuable for real-world skills.
classReview.review3Desc: Fascinating course on search algorithms and information systems. The lab sessions are very helpful.
classReview.review4Desc: Excellent graduate-level course. Deep dive into digital preservation strategies and best practices.

careerPath.title: Career Path Stories
careerPath.subtitle: Discover how our alumni have built successful careers across diverse industries with their information studies background.
careerPath.story0Title: From LibSci to Data Analyst at a Tech Startup
careerPath.story0Desc: How my information studies background gave me a unique edge in the world of data analytics and business intelligence.
careerPath.story1Title: Building a Career in Digital Preservation
careerPath.story1Desc: My journey from student to digital archivist at the National Library, preserving Thailand's cultural heritage.
careerPath.story2Title: Academic Path: From BA to PhD
careerPath.story2Desc: Pursuing research in knowledge organization and becoming a faculty member at a leading university.
careerPath.story3Desc: Applying information science principles to user experience design in the digital product industry.
